        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n#@dataclass\r\n#class TableDataLeague(TableData):\r\n#    \"\"\"Table data for a league.\"\"\"\r\n#    league: str\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass PlotData:\r\n    \"\"\"Data structure for chart/plot visualization.\"\"\"\r\n    title: str\r\n    series: List[Dict[str, Any]]\r\n    x_axis: Optional[List[Any]] = None\r\n    y_axis_label: Optional[str] = None\r\n    x_axis_label: Optional[str] = None\r\n    plot_type: str = \"line\"  # line, bar, scatter, pie, etc.\r\n    options: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    \r\n    def to_dict(self) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"Convert to a dictionary suitable for JSON serialization\"\"\"\r\n        return {\r\n            \"title\": self.title,\r\n            \"series\": self.series,\r\n            \"xAxis\": {\"categories\": self.x_axis} if self.x_axis else {},\r\n            \"yAxis\": {\"title\": {\"text\": self.y_axis_label}} if self.y_axis_label else {},\r\n            \"plotType\": self.plot_type,\r\n            \"options\": self.options\r\n        }",

        "contents": "from dataclasses import dataclass, field\r\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional, Union\r\nfrom itertools import accumulate\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass SeriesData:\r\n    \"\"\"Data structure for series data.\"\"\"\r\n    label_x_axis: str\r\n    label_y_axis: str\r\n    name: str\r\n    length: int = 0\r\n    query_params: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Store query parameters like season, league, etc.\r\n    data: Dict[str, List[float]] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    data_accumulated: Dict[str, List[float]] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    total: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    average: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)\r\n    \r\n    def to_dict(self) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:    \r\n        \"\"\"Convert to a dictionary suitable for JSON serialization\"\"\"\r\n        \r\n        sorted_by_alphabet = sorted(self.data.keys())\r\n        sorted_by_total = sorted(self.data.keys(), key=lambda x: self.total[x], reverse=True)\r\n        sorted_by_average = sorted(self.data.keys(), key=lambda x: self.average[x], reverse=True)\r\n\r\n        return {    \r\n            \"name\": self.name,\r\n            \"label_x_axis\": self.label_x_axis,\r\n            \"label_y_axis\": self.label_y_axis,\r\n            \"query_params\": self.query_params,\r\n            \"data\": self.data,   \r\n            \"data_accumulated\": self.data_accumulated,\r\n            \"total\": self.total,\r\n            \"average\": self.average,\r\n            \"length\": self.length,\r\n            \"sorted_by_alphabet\": sorted_by_alphabet,\r\n            \"sorted_by_total\": sorted_by_total,\r\n            \"sorted_by_average\": sorted_by_average\r\n\r\n        }",

        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass TableData:\r\n    \"\"\"Complete table data structure.\"\"\"\r\n    columns: List[ColumnGroup]\r\n    data: List[List[Any]]\r\n    title: Optional[str] = None\r\n    description: Optional[str] = None\r\n    config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Table-wide configuration\r\n    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=dict)  # Additional metadata for i18n and UI\r\n    row_metadata: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = field(default_factory=list)  #         "contents": "import pandas as pd\r\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, Optional, List\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns\r\nfrom app.models.series_data import SeriesData\r\n\r\ndef calculate_series_data(\r\n    data: List[Dict[str, Any]],\r\n    x_field: str,\r\n    y_field: str,\r\n    name: str,\r\n    label_x: str,\r\n    label_y: str,\r\n    query_params: Dict[str, Any]\r\n) -\u003e SeriesData:\r\n    \"\"\"Calculate series data from raw data.\"\"\"\r\n    series = SeriesData(\r\n        label_x_axis=label_x,\r\n        label_y_axis=label_y,\r\n        name=name\r\n    )\r\n    \r\n    # Store query parameters\r\n    series.query_params = query_params\r\n    \r\n    # Group data by x_field\r\n    grouped_data: Dict[str, List[float]] = {}\r\n    for item in data:\r\n        key = str(item[x_field])\r\n        if key not in grouped_data:\r\n            grouped_data[key] = []\r\n        grouped_data[key].append(float(item[y_field]))\r\n    \r\n    # Add data to series\r\n    for key, values in grouped_data.items():\r\n        series.add_data(key, values)\r\n    \r\n    return series",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "import pandas as pd\r"
          },

            "text": "def calculate_series_data(\r"

          {

            "text": "    x_field: str,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "    y_field: str,\r"

            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "    name: str,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "    label_x: str,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "    label_y: str,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "    query_params: Dict[str, Any]\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": ") -\u003e SeriesData:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "    \"\"\"Calculate series data from raw data.\"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "    series = SeriesData(\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "        label_x_axis=label_x,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "        label_y_axis=label_y,\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "        name=name\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "    )\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "    # Store query parameters\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    series.query_params = query_params\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    # Group data by x_field\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    grouped_data: Dict[str, List[float]] = {}\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    for item in data:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "        key = str(item[x_field])\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "        if key not in grouped_data:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "            grouped_data[key] = []\r"

          {

            "text": "        grouped_data[key].append(float(item[y_field]))\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 32,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "    # Add data to series\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "    for key, values in grouped_data.items():\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "        series.add_data(key, values)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "    return series"
          }

        "originalContents": "import pandas as pd\r\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, Optional, List\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns\r\nfrom app.models.series_data import SeriesData\r\n\r\ndef calculate_series_data(\r\n    data: List[Dict[str, Any]],\r\n    x_field: str,\r\n    y_field: str,\r\n    name: str,\r\n    label_x: str,\r\n    label_y: str,\r\n    query_params: Dict[str, Any]\r\n) -\u003e SeriesData:\r\n    \"\"\"Calculate series data from raw data.\"\"\"\r\n    series = SeriesData(\r\n        label_x_axis=label_x,\r\n        label_y_axis=label_y,\r\n        name=name\r\n    )\r\n    \r\n    # Store query parameters\r\n    series.query_params = query_params\r\n    \r\n    # Group data by x_field\r\n    grouped_data: Dict[str, List[float]] = {}\r\n    for item in data:\r\n        key = str(item[x_field])\r\n        if key not in grouped_data:\r\n            grouped_data[key] = []\r\n        grouped_data[key].append(float(item[y_field]))\r\n    \r\n    # Add data to series\r\n    for key, values in grouped_data.items():\r\n        series.add_data(key, values)\r\n    \r\n    return series",

        "contents": "# Team Stats Enhancement Plan\r\n\r\n## Overview\r\nThis document outlines the complete implementation plan for enhancing the team statistics page with advanced analytics and insights.\r\n\r\n## Phase 0: Foundation \u0026 Documentation\r\n- [ ] Create comprehensive data glossary and definitions\r\n- [ ] Document database schema and column meanings  \r\n- [ ] Define key metrics and calculation methods\r\n- [ ] Establish common terminology across team/player/league contexts\r\n- [ ] Document data types (input_data vs computed_data, individual vs team totals)\r\n## Phase 1: League Comparison + History ✅ COMPLETED\r\n\r\n### ✅ Backend Implementation\r\n- [x] Add &#96;get_league_comparison_data()&#96; method to TeamService\r\n- [x] Add &#96;get_league_averages()&#96; method to TeamService  \r\n- [x] Add &#96;get_team_vs_league_performance()&#96; method to TeamService\r\n- [x] Add &#96;/team/get_league_comparison&#96; API endpoint\r\n\r\n### ✅ Frontend Implementation\r\n- [x] Add \"Leistung vs. Liga-Durchschnitt\" section to team stats page\r\n- [x] Create area chart showing team vs league average with conditional coloring\r\n- [x] Create league comparison table with detailed metrics (reordered columns)\r\n- [x] Add &#96;updateLeagueComparison()&#96; JavaScript function\r\n- [x] Integrate with team selection handler\r\n- [x] Remove deprecated \"Durchschnittliche Leistung pro Saison\" chart\r\n- [x] Clean up deprecated backend functions and routes\r\n\r\n### ✅ Features Delivered\r\n- League average scores and points per season (using individual data)\r\n- Team performance vs league average (difference, Z-scores)\r\n- Historical league strength context\r\n- Visual area chart with green/red coloring for performance gaps\r\n- Clean, focused layout with full-width team history chart\r\n- Proper column ordering in comparison table\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n## Phase 2: Clutch \u0026 Consistency\r\n\r\n### Backend Implementation\r\n- [ ] Add &#96;get_clutch_performance()&#96; method to TeamService\r\n- [ ] Add &#96;get_consistency_metrics()&#96; method to TeamService\r\n- [ ] Add &#96;get_streak_analysis()&#96; method to TeamService\r\n",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\n\n    ...\n\n\n    def get_team_individual_scores_table(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\n        \"\"\"\n        Returns a table with all individual scores of each player of the selected team at the given event (league, season, week).\n        - Col group 'Opponents': Name (opponent name)\n        - Col group with team name: Score, Points, Total Points (sum of all individual points + team points for the match)\n        - One col group per player who played: Pos, Score, Points\n        - Each row is a match (round) for the team in that week\n        - Summary row at the end: sum of all individual scores and points per player, sum of team totals\n        - hbar above the final row, final row bold\n        \"\"\"\n        import pandas as pd\n        from app.models.table_data import TableData, ColumnGroup, Column\n        from data_access.schema import Columns\n\n        # Get all individual player results for the team in this event\n        player_filters = {\n            Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n            Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n            Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n            Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\n        }\n        player_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=player_filters)\n        if player_data.empty:\n            return TableData(columns=[], data=[], title=f\"No data available for {team} - Week {week}\")\n\n        # Get team totals (computed data)\n        team_filters = {\n            Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n            Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n            Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n            Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\n        }\n        team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\n    \n    ...\n\n    \n    def get_league_performance_chart(self, season: str, league: str, team_id: Optional[str] = None) -\u003e PlotData:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get a chart showing team performance over time.\n        \n        Args:\n            season: The season identifier\n            league: The league name\n            team_id: Optional team ID to highlight (if None, shows all teams)\n            \n        Returns:\n            PlotData object with team performance data\n        \"\"\"\n        # Get league standings\n        standings = self.get_league_standings(season, league)\n        \n        if not standings.teams:\n            return PlotData(\n                title=f\"No data available for {league} - {season}\",\n                series=[]\n            )\n        \n        # Get all weeks in the season\n        weeks = sorted(set(\n            perf.week for team in standings.teams \n            for perf in team.weekly_performances\n        ))\n        \n        # Prepare series data for each team\n        series = []\n        for team in standings.teams:\n            # Skip teams that aren't the highlighted team (if specified)\n            if team_id and team.team_id != team_id:\n                continue\n                \n            # Create a map of week to points for this team\n            week_to_points = {p.week: p.points for p in team.weekly_performances}\n            \n            # Calculate cumulative points for each week\n            cumulative_points = []\n            total = 0\n            for week in weeks:\n                total += week_to_points.get(week, 0)\n                cumulative_points.append(total)\n            \n            # Add this team's series\n            series.append({\n                \"name\": team.team_name,\n                \"data\": cumulative_points\n            })\n        \n        # Create and return the PlotData\n        return PlotData(\n            title=f\"{league} Team Performance - {season}\",\n            series=series,\n            x_axis=weeks,\n            y_axis_label=\"Cumulative Points\",\n            x_axis_label=\"Week\",\n            plot_type=\"line\"\n        )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "class LeagueService:"

            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 261.5,

            "lineNumber": 319,
            "text": "            y_axis_label=\"Cumulative Points\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 320,
            "text": "            x_axis_label=\"Week\","
          },

            "lineNumber": 321,

            "lineNumber": 322,
            "text": "        )"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n    \n    def get_league_performance_chart(self, season: str, league: str, team_id: Optional[str] = None) -\u003e PlotData:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get a chart showing team performance over time.\n        \n        Args:\n            season: The season identifier\n            league: The league name\n            team_id: Optional team ID to highlight (if None, shows all teams)\n            \n        Returns:\n            PlotData object with team performance data\n        \"\"\"\n        # Get league standings\n        standings = self.get_league_standings(season, league)\n        \n        if not standings.teams:\n            return PlotData(\n                title=f\"No data available for {league} - {season}\",\n                series=[]\n            )\n        \n        # Get all weeks in the season\n        weeks = sorted(set(\n            perf.week for team in standings.teams \n            for perf in team.weekly_performances\n        ))\n        \n        # Prepare series data for each team\n        series = []\n        for team in standings.teams:\n            # Skip teams that aren't the highlighted team (if specified)\n            if team_id and team.team_id != team_id:\n                continue\n                \n            # Create a map of week to points for this team\n            week_to_points = {p.week: p.points for p in team.weekly_performances}\n            \n            # Calculate cumulative points for each week\n            cumulative_points = []\n            total = 0\n            for week in weeks:\n                total += week_to_points.get(week, 0)\n                cumulative_points.append(total)\n            \n            # Add this team's series\n            series.append({\n                \"name\": team.team_name,\n                \"data\": cumulative_points\n            })\n        \n        # Create and return the PlotData\n        return PlotData(\n            title=f\"{league} Team Performance - {season}\",\n            series=series,\n            x_axis=weeks,\n            y_axis_label=\"Cumulative Points\",\n            x_axis_label=\"Week\",\n            plot_type=\"line\"\n        )",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 10,
            "line": 322
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 10,
            "line": 262
          }

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\n\n    ...\n\n\n    def get_league_history_table_data(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: Optional[int] = None) -\u003e TableData:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get league history as a TableData object for rendering.\n        \n        Args:\n            league_name: The league name\n            season: The season identifier\n            week: The week number (if None, gets the latest)\n            \n        Returns:\n            TableData object with the league history\n        \"\"\"\n        return self.get_league_table_simple(season=season, league=league_name, week=week, include_history=True)\n\n    def get_team_averages_simple(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Get team averages throughout a season - simple direct query approach\"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Direct query to get team data for the league and season\n            filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'},\n\n            }\n            \n            # Get all team data for this league and season\n            team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=filters)\n            \n            if team_data.empty:\n                return SeriesData(\n                    label_x_axis=\"Spieltag\", \n                    label_y_axis=\"Durchschnitt\", \n                    name=\"Durchschnitt im Saisonverlauf\", \n                    query_params={\"season\": season, \"league\": league_name, \"computed_data\": False}\n                ).to_dict()\n            \n            # Group by team and week to calculate averages\n            series_data = SeriesData(\n                label_x_axis=\"Spieltag\", \n                label_y_axis=\"Durchschnitt\", \n                name=\"Durchschnitt im Saisonverlauf\", \n                query_params={\"season\": season, \"league\": league_name}\n            )\n            \n            # Get all teams and weeks\n            teams = team_data[Columns.team_name].unique()",

        "originalContents": "\n\n    def get_league_history_table_data(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: Optional[int] = None) -\u003e TableData:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get league history as a TableData object for rendering.\n        \n        Args:\n            league_name: The league name\n            season: The season identifier\n            week: The week number (if None, gets the latest)\n            \n        Returns:\n            TableData object with the league history\n        \"\"\"\n        return self.get_league_table_simple(season=season, league=league_name, week=week, include_history=True)\n\n    def get_team_averages_simple(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Get team averages throughout a season - simple direct query approach\"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Direct query to get team data for the league and season\n            filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'},\n\n            }\n            \n            # Get all team data for this league and season\n            team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=filters)\n            \n            if team_data.empty:\n                return SeriesData(\n                    label_x_axis=\"Spieltag\", \n                    label_y_axis=\"Durchschnitt\", \n                    name=\"Durchschnitt im Saisonverlauf\", \n                    query_params={\"season\": season, \"league\": league_name, \"computed_data\": False}\n                ).to_dict()\n            \n            # Group by team and week to calculate averages\n            series_data = SeriesData(\n                label_x_axis=\"Spieltag\", \n                label_y_axis=\"Durchschnitt\", \n                name=\"Durchschnitt im Saisonverlauf\", \n                query_params={\"season\": season, \"league\": league_name}\n            )\n            \n            # Get all teams and weeks\n            teams = team_data[Columns.team_name].unique()",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n        } catch (error) {\r\n            console.error('Error creating cumulated chart:', error);\r\n            document.getElementById(&#96;${containerId}-chart-cumulated-content&#96;).innerHTML = &#96;\r\n                \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                    \u003cstrong\u003eNote:\u003c/strong\u003e Data is not in a format that can be displayed as a chart.\r\n                \u003c/div\u003e\r\n            &#96;;\r\n        }\r\n    });\r\n}\r\n\r\n// Create a generic table from JSON data\r\nfunction createGenericTable(containerId, data) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(containerId);\r\n    \r\n    if (Array.isArray(data)) {\r\n        // Array of objects\r\n        if (data.length \u003e 0 \u0026\u0026 typeof data[0] === 'object') {\r\n            const columns = Object.keys(data[0]);\r\n            const tableData = {\r\n                columns: columns,\r\n                data: data.map(item =\u003e columns.map(col =\u003e item[col]))\r\n            };\r\n            createTableBootstrap3(containerId, tableData);\r\n        } else {\r\n            // Simple array\r\n            const tableData = {\r\n                columns: ['Index', 'Value'],\r\n                data: data.map((item, index) =\u003e [index, item])\r\n            };\r\n            createTableBootstrap3(containerId, tableData);\r\n        }\r\n    } else if (typeof data === 'object') {\r\n        // Object\r\n        const tableData = {\r\n            columns: ['Key', 'Value'],\r\n            data: Object.entries(data).map(([key, value]) =\u003e [\r\n                key, \r\n                typeof value === 'object' ? JSON.stringify(value) : value\r\n            ])\r\n        };\r\n        createTableBootstrap3(containerId, tableData);\r\n    } else {\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cstrong\u003eNote:\u003c/strong\u003e Data is not in a format that can be displayed as a table.\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    }\r\n}\r\n\r\n// Create a chart from JSON data\r\n",

        "originalContents": "\r\n        } catch (error) {\r\n            console.error('Error creating cumulated chart:', error);\r\n            document.getElementById(&#96;${containerId}-chart-cumulated-content&#96;).innerHTML = &#96;\r\n                \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                    \u003cstrong\u003eNote:\u003c/strong\u003e Data is not in a format that can be displayed as a chart.\r\n                \u003c/div\u003e\r\n            &#96;;\r\n        }\r\n    });\r\n}\r\n\r\n// Create a generic table from JSON data\r\nfunction createGenericTable(containerId, data) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(containerId);\r\n    \r\n    if (Array.isArray(data)) {\r\n        // Array of objects\r\n        if (data.length \u003e 0 \u0026\u0026 typeof data[0] === 'object') {\r\n            const columns = Object.keys(data[0]);\r\n            const tableData = {\r\n                columns: columns,\r\n                data: data.map(item =\u003e columns.map(col =\u003e item[col]))\r\n            };\r\n            createTableBootstrap3(containerId, tableData);\r\n        } else {\r\n            // Simple array\r\n            const tableData = {\r\n                columns: ['Index', 'Value'],\r\n                data: data.map((item, index) =\u003e [index, item])\r\n            };\r\n            createTableBootstrap3(containerId, tableData);\r\n        }\r\n    } else if (typeof data === 'object') {\r\n        // Object\r\n        const tableData = {\r\n            columns: ['Key', 'Value'],\r\n            data: Object.entries(data).map(([key, value]) =\u003e [\r\n                key, \r\n                typeof value === 'object' ? JSON.stringify(value) : value\r\n            ])\r\n        };\r\n        createTableBootstrap3(containerId, tableData);\r\n    } else {\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cstrong\u003eNote:\u003c/strong\u003e Data is not in a format that can be displayed as a table.\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    }\r\n}\r\n\r\n// Create a chart from JSON data\r\n",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\n\n    ...\n\n\n    def get_league_table_simple(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None, include_history: bool = False) -\u003e TableData:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get a simplified league table with direct data query.\n        Can handle both single week and multiple weeks (history).\n        \n        Args:\n            season: The season identifier\n            league: The league name\n            week: The current week (if None, gets the latest)\n            include_history: If True, shows all weeks up to the selected week\n            \n        Returns:\n            TableData object with the league standings\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # If week is not specified, get the latest week\n            if week is None:\n                week = self.get_latest_week(season, league)\n            \n            # Direct query to get all data for this league and season (both individual and team points)\n            # Get individual player data (for scores and averages)\n            individual_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            individual_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=individual_filters)\n            \n            # Get team bonus data (for team points)\n            team_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            team_bonus_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\n            \n            # Use individual data for the main league data (scores and averages)\n            league_data = individual_data",

        "contents": "\u003cscript src=\"https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\r\n\u003cscript src=\"https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/echarts@5.6.0/dist/echarts.min.js\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\r\n\r\n\u003cscript\u003e\r\n// --- Team Color Palettes ---\r\nconst TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES = {\r\n  \"harmonic10\": [\r\n    \"#1F77B4\", \"#FF7F0E\", \"#2CA02C\", \"#D62728\", \"#9467BD\",\r\n    \"#8C564B\", \"#E377C2\", \"#7F7F7F\", \"#BCBD22\", \"#17BECF\"\r\n  ],\r\n  \"rainbowPastel\": [\r\n    \"#1B8CA6\", \"#2CA89A\", \"#8CBF8A\", \"#E6C86E\", \"#F7A86E\",\r\n    \"#E86E56\", \"#D95A6A\", \"#C94C8A\", \"#A04CBF\", \"#D6A4E6\"\r\n  ]\r\n};\r\n\r\n// Default palette\r\nlet currentPaletteName = \"harmonic10\";\r\nlet currentPalette = TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES[currentPaletteName];\r\n\r\nlet teamColorMap = {};\r\n\r\nfunction updateTeamColorMap(currentTeams) {\r\n  // Remove teams no longer present\r\n  Object.keys(teamColorMap).forEach(team =\u003e {\r\n    if (!currentTeams.includes(team)) {\r\n      delete teamColorMap[team];\r\n    }\r\n  });\r\n\r\n  // Assign colors to new teams\r\n  const usedColors = Object.values(teamColorMap);\r\n  let paletteIdx = 0;\r\n  currentTeams.forEach(team =\u003e {\r\n    if (!teamColorMap[team]) {\r\n      // Find the next unused color in the palette\r\n      while (paletteIdx \u003c currentPalette.length \u0026\u0026 usedColors.includes(currentPalette[paletteIdx])) {\r\n        paletteIdx++;\r\n      }\r\n      // If we run out of unique colors, start reusing\r\n      teamColorMap[team] = currentPalette[paletteIdx % currentPalette.length];\r\n      paletteIdx++;\r\n    }\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction getTeamColor(team) {\r\n  return teamColorMap[team] || \"#888\"; // fallback color\r\n}\r\n\r\n// --- Palette Switcher UI ---\r\n",

        "originalContents": "\u003cscript src=\"https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\r\n\u003cscript src=\"https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/echarts@5.6.0/dist/echarts.min.js\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\r\n\r\n\u003cscript\u003e\r\n// --- Team Color Palettes ---\r\nconst TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES = {\r\n  \"harmonic10\": [\r\n    \"#1F77B4\", \"#FF7F0E\", \"#2CA02C\", \"#D62728\", \"#9467BD\",\r\n    \"#8C564B\", \"#E377C2\", \"#7F7F7F\", \"#BCBD22\", \"#17BECF\"\r\n  ],\r\n  \"rainbowPastel\": [\r\n    \"#1B8CA6\", \"#2CA89A\", \"#8CBF8A\", \"#E6C86E\", \"#F7A86E\",\r\n    \"#E86E56\", \"#D95A6A\", \"#C94C8A\", \"#A04CBF\", \"#D6A4E6\"\r\n  ]\r\n};\r\n\r\n// Default palette\r\nlet currentPaletteName = \"harmonic10\";\r\nlet currentPalette = TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES[currentPaletteName];\r\n\r\nlet teamColorMap = {};\r\n\r\nfunction updateTeamColorMap(currentTeams) {\r\n  // Remove teams no longer present\r\n  Object.keys(teamColorMap).forEach(team =\u003e {\r\n    if (!currentTeams.includes(team)) {\r\n      delete teamColorMap[team];\r\n    }\r\n  });\r\n\r\n  // Assign colors to new teams\r\n  const usedColors = Object.values(teamColorMap);\r\n  let paletteIdx = 0;\r\n  currentTeams.forEach(team =\u003e {\r\n    if (!teamColorMap[team]) {\r\n      // Find the next unused color in the palette\r\n      while (paletteIdx \u003c currentPalette.length \u0026\u0026 usedColors.includes(currentPalette[paletteIdx])) {\r\n        paletteIdx++;\r\n      }\r\n      // If we run out of unique colors, start reusing\r\n      teamColorMap[team] = currentPalette[paletteIdx % currentPalette.length];\r\n      paletteIdx++;\r\n    }\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction getTeamColor(team) {\r\n  return teamColorMap[team] || \"#888\"; // fallback color\r\n}\r\n\r\n// --- Palette Switcher UI ---\r\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "document.addEventListener(\"DOMContentLoaded\", function() {\r\n  renderPaletteSwitcher();\r\n  // Palette dropdown in navbar\r\n  document.querySelectorAll('.palette-option').forEach(function(el) {\r\n    el.addEventListener('click', function(e) {\r\n      e.preventDefault();\r\n      const palette = el.getAttribute('data-palette');\r\n      if (palette \u0026\u0026 TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES[palette]) {\r\n        currentPaletteName = palette;\r\n        currentPalette = TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES[palette];\r\n        // Update navbar text\r\n        const text = el.textContent.trim();\r\n        const paletteText = document.getElementById('currentPaletteText');\r\n        if (paletteText) paletteText.textContent = text;\r\n        if (typeof window.refreshAllCharts === \"function\") window.refreshAllCharts();\r\n      }\r\n    });\r\n  });\r\n});\r\n\r\n// --- NOTE: Add \u003cdiv id=\"paletteSwitcher\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e to your HTML where you want the palette switcher to appear. ---\r\n\r\n// --- Update all chart color assignments below ---\r\n\r\n// Example for createLineChart:\r\nfunction createLineChart(data, order, containerId, title, labels, invertYAxis = false, yAxisRange = null) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(containerId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error('createLineChart: Container not found:', containerId);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Coerce invertYAxis to boolean and log\r\n    const originalInvertYAxis = invertYAxis;\r\n    invertYAxis = (invertYAxis === true || invertYAxis === 'true');\r\n    console.log('createLineChart invertYAxis argument:', originalInvertYAxis, '-\u003e coerced:', invertYAxis, 'type:', typeof invertYAxis);\r\n\r\n    // Dispose of existing chart instance if it exists\r\n    const existingChart = echarts.getInstanceByDom(container);\r\n    if (existingChart) {\r\n        existingChart.dispose();\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (order == null) {\r\n        order = Object.keys(data);\r\n    }\r\n    // Update teamColorMap for this set of teams\r\n    updateTeamColorMap(order);\r\n",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "      }\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "    });\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "  });\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "});\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "// --- NOTE: Add \u003cdiv id=\"paletteSwitcher\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e to your HTML where you want the palette switcher to appear. ---\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "// --- Update all chart color assignments below ---\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "// Example for createLineChart:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "function createLineChart(data, order, containerId, title, labels, invertYAxis = false, yAxisRange = null) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "    const container = document.getElementById(containerId);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "    if (!container) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "        console.error('createLineChart: Container not found:', containerId);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "        return;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "    }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "    // Coerce invertYAxis to boolean and log\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "    const originalInvertYAxis = invertYAxis;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "    invertYAxis = (invertYAxis === true || invertYAxis === 'true');\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "    console.log('createLineChart invertYAxis argument:', originalInvertYAxis, '-\u003e coerced:', invertYAxis, 'type:', typeof invertYAxis);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "    // Dispose of existing chart instance if it exists\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "    const existingChart = echarts.getInstanceByDom(container);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "    if (existingChart) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "        existingChart.dispose();\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "    }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": "    if (order == null) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": "        order = Object.keys(data);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129,
            "text": "    }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 130,
            "text": "    // Update teamColorMap for this set of teams\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131,
            "text": "    updateTeamColorMap(order);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "document.addEventListener(\"DOMContentLoaded\", function() {\r\n  renderPaletteSwitcher();\r\n  // Palette dropdown in navbar\r\n  document.querySelectorAll('.palette-option').forEach(function(el) {\r\n    el.addEventListener('click', function(e) {\r\n      e.preventDefault();\r\n      const palette = el.getAttribute('data-palette');\r\n      if (palette \u0026\u0026 TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES[palette]) {\r\n        currentPaletteName = palette;\r\n        currentPalette = TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES[palette];\r\n        // Update navbar text\r\n        const text = el.textContent.trim();\r\n        const paletteText = document.getElementById('currentPaletteText');\r\n        if (paletteText) paletteText.textContent = text;\r\n        if (typeof window.refreshAllCharts === \"function\") window.refreshAllCharts();\r\n      }\r\n    });\r\n  });\r\n});\r\n\r\n// --- NOTE: Add \u003cdiv id=\"paletteSwitcher\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e to your HTML where you want the palette switcher to appear. ---\r\n\r\n// --- Update all chart color assignments below ---\r\n\r\n// Example for createLineChart:\r\nfunction createLineChart(data, order, containerId, title, labels, invertYAxis = false, yAxisRange = null) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(containerId);\r\n    if (!container) {\r\n        console.error('createLineChart: Container not found:', containerId);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Coerce invertYAxis to boolean and log\r\n    const originalInvertYAxis = invertYAxis;\r\n    invertYAxis = (invertYAxis === true || invertYAxis === 'true');\r\n    console.log('createLineChart invertYAxis argument:', originalInvertYAxis, '-\u003e coerced:', invertYAxis, 'type:', typeof invertYAxis);\r\n\r\n    // Dispose of existing chart instance if it exists\r\n    const existingChart = echarts.getInstanceByDom(container);\r\n    if (existingChart) {\r\n        existingChart.dispose();\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    if (order == null) {\r\n        order = Object.keys(data);\r\n    }\r\n    // Update teamColorMap for this set of teams\r\n    updateTeamColorMap(order);\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function renderPaletteSwitcher(containerId = \"paletteSwitcher\") {\r\n  const container = document.getElementById(containerId);\r\n  if (!container) return;\r\n  container.innerHTML = \"\u003cb\u003eColor Palette:\u003c/b\u003e \";\r\n  Object.keys(TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES).forEach(name =\u003e {\r\n    const btn = document.createElement(\"button\");\r\n    btn.textContent = name.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \" $1\").replace(/^./, s =\u003e s.toUpperCase());\r\n    btn.onclick = () =\u003e {\r\n      currentPaletteName = name;\r\n      currentPalette = TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES[name];\r\n      if (typeof window.refreshAllCharts === \"function\") window.refreshAllCharts();\r\n    };\r\n    btn.style.margin = \"0 4px\";\r\n    btn.style.padding = \"4px 10px\";\r\n    btn.style.borderRadius = \"4px\";\r\n    btn.style.border = name === currentPaletteName ? \"2px solid #222\" : \"1px solid #aaa\";\r\n    btn.style.background = name === currentPaletteName ? \"#eee\" : \"#fff\";\r\n    btn.style.cursor = \"pointer\";\r\n    container.appendChild(btn);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\n// --- Global chart refresh stub (user should implement) ---\r\nif (!window.refreshAllCharts) {\r\n  window.refreshAllCharts = function() {\r\n    // User: call your chart rendering functions here to update colors\r\n    // Example: updateTableLeagueWeek(); updateTablePosition(data); etc.\r\n    // console.log('refreshAllCharts called');\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\n// --- Palette Dropdown Handler for Navbar ---\r\n",

        "originalContents": "function renderPaletteSwitcher(containerId = \"paletteSwitcher\") {\r\n  const container = document.getElementById(containerId);\r\n  if (!container) return;\r\n  container.innerHTML = \"\u003cb\u003eColor Palette:\u003c/b\u003e \";\r\n  Object.keys(TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES).forEach(name =\u003e {\r\n    const btn = document.createElement(\"button\");\r\n    btn.textContent = name.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \" $1\").replace(/^./, s =\u003e s.toUpperCase());\r\n    btn.onclick = () =\u003e {\r\n      currentPaletteName = name;\r\n      currentPalette = TEAM_COLOR_PALETTES[name];\r\n      if (typeof window.refreshAllCharts === \"function\") window.refreshAllCharts();\r\n    };\r\n    btn.style.margin = \"0 4px\";\r\n    btn.style.padding = \"4px 10px\";\r\n    btn.style.borderRadius = \"4px\";\r\n    btn.style.border = name === currentPaletteName ? \"2px solid #222\" : \"1px solid #aaa\";\r\n    btn.style.background = name === currentPaletteName ? \"#eee\" : \"#fff\";\r\n    btn.style.cursor = \"pointer\";\r\n    container.appendChild(btn);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\n// --- Global chart refresh stub (user should implement) ---\r\nif (!window.refreshAllCharts) {\r\n  window.refreshAllCharts = function() {\r\n    // User: call your chart rendering functions here to update colors\r\n    // Example: updateTableLeagueWeek(); updateTablePosition(data); etc.\r\n    // console.log('refreshAllCharts called');\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\n// --- Palette Dropdown Handler for Navbar ---\r\n",

        "contents": "\u003c!DOCTYPE html\u003e\r\n\u003chtml lang=\"en\"\u003e\r\n\u003chead\u003e\r\n    \u003cmeta charset=\"UTF-8\"\u003e\r\n    \u003cmeta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\"\u003e\r\n    \u003ctitle\u003eBowl-A-Lyzer\u003c/title\u003e\r\n    \u003c!-- Bootstrap CSS --\u003e\r\n    \u003clink href=\"https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.3.3/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css\" rel=\"stylesheet\"\u003e\r\n    \u003c!-- Custom CSS --\u003e\r\n    \u003cstyle\u003e\r\n        :root {\r\n            /* Color Palette */\r\n            --color-green-light: #a1e8c4;   /* Lightest green */\r\n            --color-green: #86e1b3;         /* Light green */\r\n            --color-blue-lightest: #7dcfe6; /* Light blue-green */\r\n            --color-blue-light: #4aa8c2;    /* Light blue */\r\n            --color-blue: #0a9dc7;          /* Dark blue */\r\n\r\n\r\n            /* Semantic Color Assignments */\r\n            --theme-primary: var(--color-blue);         /* Main UI elements like navbar, card headers */\r\n            --theme-secondary: var(--color-blue-light); /* Buttons, interactive elements */\r\n            --theme-accent: var(--color-blue-lightest); /* Highlights, accents */\r\n            --theme-warning: var(--color-green);        /* Warning states */\r\n            --theme-info: var(--color-green-light);     /* Info states */\r\n            \r\n            /* Background Colors */\r\n            --theme-background: #f8f9fa;\r\n            --theme-surface: #ffffff;\r\n            \r\n            /* Text Colors */\r\n            --theme-text-on-primary: #ffffff;\r\n            --theme-text-on-secondary: #ffffff;\r\n            --theme-text-on-light: var(--color-blue);\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        body {\r\n            background-color: var(--theme-background);\r\n            color: var(--theme-text-on-light);\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        .navbar {\r\n            background-color: var(--theme-primary) !important;\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        .navbar-brand, .nav-link {\r\n            color: var(--theme-text-on-primary) !important;\r\n        }\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [

            "text": "        :root {\r"

            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "            /* Color Palette */\r"

            "text": "            /* Semantic Color Assignments */\r"

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\u003clink href=\"https://unpkg.com/tabulator-tables@6.3.0/dist/css/tabulator.min.css\" rel=\"stylesheet\"\u003e\r\n\u003cscript type=\"text/javascript\" src=\"https://unpkg.com/tabulator-tables@6.3.0/dist/js/tabulator.min.js\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\r\n\r\n\u003cscript\u003e\r\nfunction createTable(config) {\r\n    const {\r\n        data,\r\n        columns,\r\n        headerGroups = [],  // This contains the actual colspan values\r\n        rowNumbering = true,\r\n        positionChange = false\r\n    } = config;\r\n\r\n    let currentData = [...data];\r\n\r\n    // Get colors for groups using #2196F3 as end color\r\n    const getGroupColors = () =\u003e {\r\n        const endColor = '#7ec2f8';  // Bootstrap primary blue\r\n        const startRGB = [255, 255, 255];  // white\r\n        const endRGB = hexToRgb(endColor);\r\n        \r\n        return headerGroups.map((_, index) =\u003e {\r\n            if (index === 0) return 'rgb(255, 255, 255)';  // First group is white\r\n            if (index === headerGroups.length - 1) return endColor;  // Last group is primary blue\r\n            \r\n            const factor = index / (headerGroups.length - 1);\r\n            const r = Math.round(startRGB[0] + (endRGB[0] - startRGB[0]) * factor);\r\n            const g = Math.round(startRGB[1] + (endRGB[1] - startRGB[1]) * factor);\r\n            const b = Math.round(startRGB[2] + (endRGB[2] - startRGB[2]) * factor);\r\n            \r\n            return &#96;rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})&#96;;\r\n        });\r\n    };\r\n\r\n    const hexToRgb = (hex) =\u003e {\r\n        hex = hex.replace(/^#/, '');\r\n        const bigint = parseInt(hex, 16);\r\n        const r = (bigint \u003e\u003e 16) \u0026 255;\r\n        const g = (bigint \u003e\u003e 8) \u0026 255;\r\n        const b = bigint \u0026 255;\r\n        return [r, g, b];\r\n    };\r\n\r\n    const colors = getGroupColors();\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [

            "text": "    // Get colors for groups using #2196F3 as end color\r"

        "originalContents": "\u003clink href=\"https://unpkg.com/tabulator-tables@6.3.0/dist/css/tabulator.min.css\" rel=\"stylesheet\"\u003e\r\n\u003cscript type=\"text/javascript\" src=\"https://unpkg.com/tabulator-tables@6.3.0/dist/js/tabulator.min.js\"\u003e\u003c/script\u003e\r\n\r\n\u003cscript\u003e\r\nfunction createTable(config) {\r\n    const {\r\n        data,\r\n        columns,\r\n        headerGroups = [],  // This contains the actual colspan values\r\n        rowNumbering = true,\r\n        positionChange = false\r\n    } = config;\r\n\r\n    let currentData = [...data];\r\n\r\n    // Get colors for groups using #2196F3 as end color\r\n    const getGroupColors = () =\u003e {\r\n        const endColor = '#7ec2f8';  // Bootstrap primary blue\r\n        const startRGB = [255, 255, 255];  // white\r\n        const endRGB = hexToRgb(endColor);\r\n        \r\n        return headerGroups.map((_, index) =\u003e {\r\n            if (index === 0) return 'rgb(255, 255, 255)';  // First group is white\r\n            if (index === headerGroups.length - 1) return endColor;  // Last group is primary blue\r\n            \r\n            const factor = index / (headerGroups.length - 1);\r\n            const r = Math.round(startRGB[0] + (endRGB[0] - startRGB[0]) * factor);\r\n            const g = Math.round(startRGB[1] + (endRGB[1] - startRGB[1]) * factor);\r\n            const b = Math.round(startRGB[2] + (endRGB[2] - startRGB[2]) * factor);\r\n            \r\n            return &#96;rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})&#96;;\r\n        });\r\n    };\r\n\r\n    const hexToRgb = (hex) =\u003e {\r\n        hex = hex.replace(/^#/, '');\r\n        const bigint = parseInt(hex, 16);\r\n        const r = (bigint \u003e\u003e 16) \u0026 255;\r\n        const g = (bigint \u003e\u003e 8) \u0026 255;\r\n        const b = bigint \u0026 255;\r\n        return [r, g, b];\r\n    };\r\n\r\n    const colors = getGroupColors();\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n    // Calculate y-axis range if requested\r\n    let yMin, yMax;\r\n    if (yAxisRange === 'auto') {\r\n        const allValues = order.map(team =\u003e data[team]).flat();\r\n        yMin = Math.max(Math.min(...allValues) - 10, 0);\r\n        yMax = Math.max(...allValues) + 10;\r\n    } else if (yAxisRange === 'exact') {\r\n        const allValues = order.map(team =\u003e data[team]).flat();\r\n        yMin = Math.min(...allValues);\r\n        yMax = Math.max(...allValues);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Create series from the data using the specified order\r\n    const series = order.map(team =\u003e ({\r\n        name: team,\r\n        type: 'line',\r\n        data: data[team],\r\n        lineStyle: {\r\n            color: getTeamColor(team),\r\n            width: 2\r\n        },\r\n        itemStyle: {\r\n            color: getTeamColor(team)\r\n        },\r\n        smooth: false\r\n    }));\r\n\r\n    // Chart options\r\n    const chartOptions = {\r\n        tooltip: {\r\n            trigger: 'axis'\r\n        },\r\n        legend: {\r\n            show: false\r\n        },\r\n        grid: {\r\n            top: '10%',\r\n            right: '5%',\r\n            bottom: '10%',\r\n            left: '5%',\r\n            containLabel: true\r\n        },\r\n        xAxis: {\r\n            type: 'category',\r\n            data: labels,\r\n            axisLine: { show: false },\r\n            splitLine: { show: true }\r\n        },\r\n        yAxis: {\r\n            type: 'value',\r\n            min: yMin,\r\n            max: yMax,\r\n            inverse: invertYAxis, // Use ECharts built-in inversion\r\n            axisLine: { show: true },\r\n            axisTick: { show: true },\r\n            splitLine: { show: true }\r\n        },\r\n        series: series,\r\n        animation: false\r\n    };\r\n    console.log('ECharts yAxis.inverse:', chartOptions.yAxis.inverse, 'type:', typeof chartOptions.yAxis.inverse);\r\n",

        "originalContents": "\r\n    // Calculate y-axis range if requested\r\n    let yMin, yMax;\r\n    if (yAxisRange === 'auto') {\r\n        const allValues = order.map(team =\u003e data[team]).flat();\r\n        yMin = Math.max(Math.min(...allValues) - 10, 0);\r\n        yMax = Math.max(...allValues) + 10;\r\n    } else if (yAxisRange === 'exact') {\r\n        const allValues = order.map(team =\u003e data[team]).flat();\r\n        yMin = Math.min(...allValues);\r\n        yMax = Math.max(...allValues);\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Create series from the data using the specified order\r\n    const series = order.map(team =\u003e ({\r\n        name: team,\r\n        type: 'line',\r\n        data: data[team],\r\n        lineStyle: {\r\n            color: getTeamColor(team),\r\n            width: 2\r\n        },\r\n        itemStyle: {\r\n            color: getTeamColor(team)\r\n        },\r\n        smooth: false\r\n    }));\r\n\r\n    // Chart options\r\n    const chartOptions = {\r\n        tooltip: {\r\n            trigger: 'axis'\r\n        },\r\n        legend: {\r\n            show: false\r\n        },\r\n        grid: {\r\n            top: '10%',\r\n            right: '5%',\r\n            bottom: '10%',\r\n            left: '5%',\r\n            containLabel: true\r\n        },\r\n        xAxis: {\r\n            type: 'category',\r\n            data: labels,\r\n            axisLine: { show: false },\r\n            splitLine: { show: true }\r\n        },\r\n        yAxis: {\r\n            type: 'value',\r\n            min: yMin,\r\n            max: yMax,\r\n            inverse: invertYAxis, // Use ECharts built-in inversion\r\n            axisLine: { show: true },\r\n            axisTick: { show: true },\r\n            splitLine: { show: true }\r\n        },\r\n        series: series,\r\n        animation: false\r\n    };\r\n    console.log('ECharts yAxis.inverse:', chartOptions.yAxis.inverse, 'type:', typeof chartOptions.yAxis.inverse);\r\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function createScatterChart_vanilla(data, chartId, title, axis_labels) {\r\n    const canvas = document.getElementById(chartId);\r\n    if (!canvas) {\r\n        console.error('createScatterChart_vanilla: Canvas not found:', chartId);\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\r\n    // Destroy existing chart if it exists\r\n    if (window[chartId + 'Instance'] instanceof Chart) {\r\n        window[chartId + 'Instance'].destroy();\r\n    }\r\n    // Collect all points for regression\r\n    const allPoints = Object.values(data).flat();\r\n    const xs = allPoints.map(point =\u003e point[0]);\r\n    const ys = allPoints.map(point =\u003e point[1]);\r\n    // Calculate regression line\r\n    const n = xs.length;\r\n    const sumX = xs.reduce((a, b) =\u003e a + b, 0);\r\n    const sumY = ys.reduce((a, b) =\u003e a + b, 0);\r\n    const sumXY = xs.reduce((sum, x, i) =\u003e sum + x * ys[i], 0);\r\n    const sumXX = xs.reduce((sum, x) =\u003e sum + x * x, 0);\r\n    const slope = (n * sumXY - sumX * sumY) / (n * sumXX - sumX * sumX);\r\n    const intercept = (sumY - slope * sumX) / n;\r\n    // Create regression line points\r\n    const minX = Math.min(...xs);\r\n    const maxX = Math.max(...xs);\r\n    const regressionLine = {\r\n        label: 'Regression',\r\n        data: [\r\n            {x: minX, y: slope * minX + intercept},\r\n            {x: maxX, y: slope * maxX + intercept}\r\n        ],\r\n        type: 'line',\r\n        borderColor: '#000000',\r\n        borderWidth: 1,\r\n        borderDash: [5, 5],\r\n        pointRadius: 0,\r\n        fill: false\r\n    };\r\n    // Create datasets for each team using palette colors\r\n    const teams = Object.keys(data);\r\n    const datasets = teams.map((team, idx) =\u003e ({\r\n        label: team,\r\n        data: data[team].map(point =\u003e ({x: point[0], y: point[1]})),\r\n        borderColor: getTeamColor(team),\r\n        backgroundColor: getTeamColor(team),\r\n        pointRadius: 5\r\n    }));\r\n    // Add regression line to datasets\r\n    datasets.push(regressionLine);\r\n",

        "contents": "function updateSharedLegend(teams) {\r\n    const legendHtml = teams.map(team =\u003e &#96;\r\n        \u003cdiv class=\"mx-3 mb-2 d-flex align-items-center\"\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv style=\"width: 20px; height: 20px; border-radius: 50%; background-color: ${getTeamColor(team)}; margin-right: 8px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; color: white; font-weight: bold;\"\u003e\r\n                \r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n            \u003cspan\u003e${team}\u003c/span\u003e\r\n        \u003c/div\u003e\r\n    &#96;).join('');\r\n    \r\n    document.getElementById('sharedLegend').innerHTML = legendHtml;\r\n    document.getElementById('sharedLegendMiddle').innerHTML = legendHtml;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction updatePositionChart() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const currentWeek = document.querySelector('input[name=\"week\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    \r\n",

        "originalContents": "function updateSharedLegend(teams) {\r\n    const legendHtml = teams.map(team =\u003e &#96;\r\n        \u003cdiv class=\"mx-3 mb-2 d-flex align-items-center\"\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv style=\"width: 20px; height: 20px; border-radius: 50%; background-color: ${getTeamColor(team)}; margin-right: 8px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; color: white; font-weight: bold;\"\u003e\r\n                \r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n            \u003cspan\u003e${team}\u003c/span\u003e\r\n        \u003c/div\u003e\r\n    &#96;).join('');\r\n    \r\n    document.getElementById('sharedLegend').innerHTML = legendHtml;\r\n    document.getElementById('sharedLegendMiddle').innerHTML = legendHtml;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction updatePositionChart() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const currentWeek = document.querySelector('input[name=\"week\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    \r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "const getGroupColors = (headerGroups) =\u003e {\r\n    const endColor = '#2196F3';  // Bootstrap primary blue\r\n    const startRGB = [255, 255, 255];  // white\r\n    const endRGB = hexToRgb(endColor);\r\n    \r\n    return headerGroups.map((_, index) =\u003e {\r\n        if (index === 0) return 'rgb(255, 255, 255)';  // First group is white\r\n        if (index === headerGroups.length - 1) return endColor;  // Last group is primary blue\r\n        \r\n        const factor = index / (headerGroups.length - 1);\r\n        const r = Math.round(startRGB[0] + (endRGB[0] - startRGB[0]) * factor);\r\n        const g = Math.round(startRGB[1] + (endRGB[1] - startRGB[1]) * factor);\r\n        const b = Math.round(startRGB[2] + (endRGB[2] - startRGB[2]) * factor);\r\n        \r\n        return &#96;rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})&#96;;\r\n    });\r\n};\r\n\r\nfunction createTableTabulator(tableId, data) {\r\n    // Check if the data has columns and data\r\n    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {\r\n        console.error('Invalid data format');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Transform the data from array format to object format\r\n    const transformedData = data.data.map(row =\u003e {\r\n        const obj = {};\r\n        let index = 0; // Initialize index to track the position in the row array\r\n\r\n        // Loop through each column group\r\n        data.columns.forEach(colGroup =\u003e {\r\n            if (colGroup.columns) {\r\n                // Loop through each column in the group\r\n                colGroup.columns.forEach(col =\u003e {\r\n                    obj[col.field] = row[index]; // Assign the value from the row to the object\r\n                    index++; // Increment index for the next field\r\n                });\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n        return obj; // Return the transformed object\r\n    });\r\n",

          {

        ],
        "originalContents": "const getGroupColors = (headerGroups) =\u003e {\r\n    const endColor = '#2196F3';  // Bootstrap primary blue\r\n    const startRGB = [255, 255, 255];  // white\r\n    const endRGB = hexToRgb(endColor);\r\n    \r\n    return headerGroups.map((_, index) =\u003e {\r\n        if (index === 0) return 'rgb(255, 255, 255)';  // First group is white\r\n        if (index === headerGroups.length - 1) return endColor;  // Last group is primary blue\r\n        \r\n        const factor = index / (headerGroups.length - 1);\r\n        const r = Math.round(startRGB[0] + (endRGB[0] - startRGB[0]) * factor);\r\n        const g = Math.round(startRGB[1] + (endRGB[1] - startRGB[1]) * factor);\r\n        const b = Math.round(startRGB[2] + (endRGB[2] - startRGB[2]) * factor);\r\n        \r\n        return &#96;rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})&#96;;\r\n    });\r\n};\r\n\r\nfunction createTableTabulator(tableId, data) {\r\n    // Check if the data has columns and data\r\n    if (!data || !data.columns || !data.data) {\r\n        console.error('Invalid data format');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Transform the data from array format to object format\r\n    const transformedData = data.data.map(row =\u003e {\r\n        const obj = {};\r\n        let index = 0; // Initialize index to track the position in the row array\r\n\r\n        // Loop through each column group\r\n        data.columns.forEach(colGroup =\u003e {\r\n            if (colGroup.columns) {\r\n                // Loop through each column in the group\r\n                colGroup.columns.forEach(col =\u003e {\r\n                    obj[col.field] = row[index]; // Assign the value from the row to the object\r\n                    index++; // Increment index for the next field\r\n                });\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n        return obj; // Return the transformed object\r\n    });\r\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "} else {\r\n            // Simple array\r\n            const chartData = {\r\n                labels: data.map((_, index) =\u003e &#96;Item ${index + 1}&#96;),\r\n                datasets: [{\r\n                    label: 'Value',\r\n                    data: data,\r\n                    backgroundColor: 'rgba(54, 162, 235, 0.5)',\r\n                    borderColor: 'rgba(54, 162, 235, 1)',\r\n                    borderWidth: 1\r\n                }]\r\n            };\r\n            \r\n            const ctx = document.createElement('canvas');\r\n            container.appendChild(ctx);\r\n            \r\n            new Chart(ctx, {\r\n                type: 'bar',\r\n                data: chartData,\r\n                options: {\r\n                    responsive: true,\r\n                    maintainAspectRatio: false,\r\n                    scales: {\r\n                        y: {\r\n                            beginAtZero: true\r\n                        }\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            });\r\n        }\r\n    } else {\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cstrong\u003eNote:\u003c/strong\u003e Data is not in a format that can be displayed as a chart.\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    }\r\n}\r\n\r\n// Get random color for charts\r\nfunction getRandomColor(alpha = 1) {\r\n    const r = Math.floor(Math.random() * 256);\r\n    const g = Math.floor(Math.random() * 256);\r\n    const b = Math.floor(Math.random() * 256);\r\n    return &#96;rgba(${r}, ${g}, ${b}, ${alpha})&#96;;\r\n}\r\n\r\n",

            "lineNumber": 599,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 602,

            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 608,
            "text": "// Get random color for charts\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 609,
            "text": "function getRandomColor(alpha = 1) {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 610,
            "text": "    const r = Math.floor(Math.random() * 256);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 611,
            "text": "    const g = Math.floor(Math.random() * 256);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 612,
            "text": "    const b = Math.floor(Math.random() * 256);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 613,
            "text": "    return &#96;rgba(${r}, ${g}, ${b}, ${alpha})&#96;;\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 614,
            "text": "}\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 615,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 616
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "} else {\r\n            // Simple array\r\n            const chartData = {\r\n                labels: data.map((_, index) =\u003e &#96;Item ${index + 1}&#96;),\r\n                datasets: [{\r\n                    label: 'Value',\r\n                    data: data,\r\n                    backgroundColor: 'rgba(54, 162, 235, 0.5)',\r\n                    borderColor: 'rgba(54, 162, 235, 1)',\r\n                    borderWidth: 1\r\n                }]\r\n            };\r\n            \r\n            const ctx = document.createElement('canvas');\r\n            container.appendChild(ctx);\r\n            \r\n            new Chart(ctx, {\r\n                type: 'bar',\r\n                data: chartData,\r\n                options: {\r\n                    responsive: true,\r\n                    maintainAspectRatio: false,\r\n                    scales: {\r\n                        y: {\r\n                            beginAtZero: true\r\n                        }\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            });\r\n        }\r\n    } else {\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-warning\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cstrong\u003eNote:\u003c/strong\u003e Data is not in a format that can be displayed as a chart.\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n    }\r\n}\r\n\r\n// Get random color for charts\r\nfunction getRandomColor(alpha = 1) {\r\n    const r = Math.floor(Math.random() * 256);\r\n    const g = Math.floor(Math.random() * 256);\r\n    const b = Math.floor(Math.random() * 256);\r\n    return &#96;rgba(${r}, ${g}, ${b}, ${alpha})&#96;;\r\n}\r\n\r\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": ".custom-tabulator {\r\n    border: none; /* Remove default borders */\r\n    background-color: transparent; /* Make background transparent */\r\n    font-family: 'Your Custom Font', sans-serif; /* Use your site's font */\r\n}\r\n\r\n/* Header Styles */\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-header {\r\n    background-color: #f8f9fa; /* Light background for header */\r\n    color: #264653; /* Dark text color */\r\n    font-weight: bold; /* Bold header text */\r\n    border-bottom: 2px solid #264653; /* Subtle border */\r\n}\r\n\r\n/* Column Styles */\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-col {\r\n    border-right: 1px solid #264653; /* Subtle column borders */\r\n}\r\n\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-col .tabulator-col-content {\r\n    padding: 10px; /* Add padding for better spacing */\r\n}\r\n\r\n/* Row Styles */\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-row {\r\n    border-bottom: 1px solid #264653; /* Subtle row borders */\r\n}\r\n\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-row.tabulator-row-even {\r\n    background-color: #f9f9f9; /* Light gray for even rows */\r\n}\r\n\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-row.tabulator-row-odd {\r\n    background-color: #ffffff; /* White for odd rows */\r\n}\r\n\r\n/* Hover Effects */\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-row:hover {\r\n    background-color: rgba(38, 70, 83, 0.1); /* Light hover effect */\r\n}\r\n\r\n/* Frozen Column Styles */\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-frozen {\r\n    background-color: #ffffff; /* Background for frozen columns */\r\n    z-index: 10; /* Ensure frozen columns are above others */\r\n}\r\n\r\n/* Scrollbar Styles */\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-tableHolder::-webkit-scrollbar {\r\n    width: 8px; /* Width of the scrollbar */\r\n}\r\n\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-tableHolder::-webkit-scrollbar-track {\r\n    background: #f1f1f1; /* Track color */\r\n}\r\n\r\n.custom-tabulator .tabulator-tableHolder::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {\r\n    background: #264653; /* Thumb color */\r\n    border-radius: 4px; /* Rounded corners */\r\n}\r\n\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function renderScatterPlot() {\r\n    // Get current selection\r\n    const season = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const league = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    if (!season || !league) {\r\n        alert('Please select a season and league.');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    fetch(&#96;/league/get_team_points_vs_average?season=${encodeURIComponent(season)}\u0026league=${encodeURIComponent(league)}&#96;)\r\n        .then(res =\u003e res.json())\r\n        .then(data =\u003e {\r\n            if (!data || typeof data !== 'object' || Array.isArray(data)) {\r\n                console.error('No valid data for scatter plot:', data);\r\n                return;\r\n            }\r\n            const axis_labels = {x: 'Points', y: 'Average'};\r\n            // Use color palette for each team\r\n            if (typeof createScatterChart_vanilla === 'function') {\r\n                // Patch: inject color into data for each team\r\n                const teams = Object.keys(data);\r\n                const coloredData = {};\r\n                teams.forEach((team, idx) =\u003e {\r\n                    coloredData[team] = data[team]; // Data is array of [x, y]\r\n                    // The color will be set in the chart function using getTeamColor(team, idx)\r\n                });\r\n                createScatterChart_vanilla(coloredData, 'chartTeamPointsVsAverage', 'Points vs. Average', axis_labels);\r\n                // Render legend after every second chart (here, always after this chart)\r\n                renderScatterLegend(teams);\r\n            } else {\r\n                console.error('createScatterChart_vanilla is not defined');\r\n            }\r\n        })\r\n        .catch(err =\u003e console.error('Error fetching scatter plot data:', err));\r\n}\r\n\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "function renderScatterPlot() {\r\n    // Get current selection\r\n    const season = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const league = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    if (!season || !league) {\r\n        alert('Please select a season and league.');\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    fetch(&#96;/league/get_team_points_vs_average?season=${encodeURIComponent(season)}\u0026league=${encodeURIComponent(league)}&#96;)\r\n        .then(res =\u003e res.json())\r\n        .then(data =\u003e {\r\n            if (!data || typeof data !== 'object' || Array.isArray(data)) {\r\n                console.error('No valid data for scatter plot:', data);\r\n                return;\r\n            }\r\n            const axis_labels = {x: 'Points', y: 'Average'};\r\n            // Use color palette for each team\r\n            if (typeof createScatterChart_vanilla === 'function') {\r\n                // Patch: inject color into data for each team\r\n                const teams = Object.keys(data);\r\n                const coloredData = {};\r\n                teams.forEach((team, idx) =\u003e {\r\n                    coloredData[team] = data[team]; // Data is array of [x, y]\r\n                    // The color will be set in the chart function using getTeamColor(team, idx)\r\n                });\r\n                createScatterChart_vanilla(coloredData, 'chartTeamPointsVsAverage', 'Points vs. Average', axis_labels);\r\n                // Render legend after every second chart (here, always after this chart)\r\n                renderScatterLegend(teams);\r\n            } else {\r\n                console.error('createScatterChart_vanilla is not defined');\r\n            }\r\n        })\r\n        .catch(err =\u003e console.error('Error fetching scatter plot data:', err));\r\n}\r\n\r\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass TileData:\r\n    \"\"\"Data structure for dashboard tiles/cards.\"\"\"\r\n    title: str\r\n    value: Any  # The primary value to display\r\n    subtitle: Optional[str] = None\r\n    trend: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None  # Trend information (value, direction, etc.)\r\n    icon: Optional[str] = None\r\n    color: Optional[str] = None  # Primary color\r\n    size: str = \"small\"  # small, medium, large, wide\r\n    type: str = \"stat\"  # stat, trend, progress, chart, info\r\n    chart_data: Optional[List[Union[int, float]]] = None  # For mini charts\r\n    link: Optional[str] = None  # URL to navigate to on click\r\n    \r\n    def to_dict(self) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"Convert to a dictionary suitable for JSON serialization\"\"\"\r\n        result = {\r\n            \"title\": self.title,\r\n            \"value\": self.value,\r\n            \"size\": self.size,\r\n            \"type\": self.type\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        if self.subtitle:\r\n            result[\"subtitle\"] = self.subtitle\r\n        \r\n        if self.trend:\r\n            result[\"trend\"] = self.trend\r\n            \r\n        if self.icon:\r\n            result[\"icon\"] = self.icon\r\n            \r\n        if self.color:\r\n            result[\"color\"] = self.color\r\n            \r\n        if self.chart_data:\r\n            result[\"chartData\"] = self.chart_data\r\n            \r\n        if self.link:\r\n            result[\"link\"] = self.link\r\n            \r\n        return result",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass TileData:\r\n    \"\"\"Data structure for dashboard tiles/cards.\"\"\"\r\n    title: str\r\n    value: Any  # The primary value to display\r\n    subtitle: Optional[str] = None\r\n    trend: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None  # Trend information (value, direction, etc.)\r\n    icon: Optional[str] = None\r\n    color: Optional[str] = None  # Primary color\r\n    size: str = \"small\"  # small, medium, large, wide\r\n    type: str = \"stat\"  # stat, trend, progress, chart, info\r\n    chart_data: Optional[List[Union[int, float]]] = None  # For mini charts\r\n    link: Optional[str] = None  # URL to navigate to on click\r\n    \r\n    def to_dict(self) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"Convert to a dictionary suitable for JSON serialization\"\"\"\r\n        result = {\r\n            \"title\": self.title,\r\n            \"value\": self.value,\r\n            \"size\": self.size,\r\n            \"type\": self.type\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        if self.subtitle:\r\n            result[\"subtitle\"] = self.subtitle\r\n        \r\n        if self.trend:\r\n            result[\"trend\"] = self.trend\r\n            \r\n        if self.icon:\r\n            result[\"icon\"] = self.icon\r\n            \r\n        if self.color:\r\n            result[\"color\"] = self.color\r\n            \r\n        if self.chart_data:\r\n            result[\"chartData\"] = self.chart_data\r\n            \r\n        if self.link:\r\n            result[\"link\"] = self.link\r\n            \r\n        return result",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function displayResult(containerId, data, isError = false) {\r\n    const container = document.getElementById(containerId);\r\n    \r\n    if (isError) {\r\n        container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"alert alert-danger\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cstrong\u003eError:\u003c/strong\u003e ${data}\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        &#96;;\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    // Create tabs for different views\r\n    container.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n        \u003cstyle\u003e\r\n            /* Custom styling for tabs with higher specificity */\r\n            #${containerId} .nav-tabs .nav-link {\r\n                color: #0a9dc7 !important; /* Blue text for all tabs */\r\n                border: 1px solid #0a9dc7 !important; /* Blue border */\r\n                border-bottom: none !important;\r\n                background-color: #f8f9fa !important; /* Light gray background */\r\n            }\r\n            #${containerId} .nav-tabs .nav-link.active {\r\n                color: white !important; /* White text for active tab */\r\n                font-weight: bold !important;\r\n                background-color: #0a9dc7 !important; /* Blue background for active tab */\r\n                border-color: #0a9dc7 !important;\r\n                border-top-width: 2px !important;\r\n            }\r\n            #${containerId} .tab-content {\r\n                border: 1px solid #0a9dc7 !important;\r\n                border-top: none !important;\r\n            }\r\n            #${containerId} .nav-tabs {\r\n                border-bottom: 1px solid #0a9dc7 !important;\r\n            }\r\n        \u003c/style\u003e\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 346,

          },

            "lineNumber": 347,
            "text": "    const container = document.getElementById(containerId);\r"

            "lineNumber": 348,

            "text": "        \u003cstyle\u003e\r"

            "lineNumber": 361,
            "text": "            /* Custom styling for tabs with higher specificity */\r"

            "text": "            #${containerId} .nav-tabs .nav-link {\r"

            "lineNumber": 371,

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 373,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 374,
            "text": "            }\r"
          },
          {

            "text": "            #${containerId} .tab-content {\r"

          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 377,

          },
          {
